identity.name: Vercel
identity.oneLiner: The AI Cloud to build, deploy, and scale AI-powered web apps — Git-push deploys on a global edge network.

sources.quote: Vercel is the AI Cloud, a unified platform for building, deploying, and scaling AI-powered applications.
Every push to your connected branch triggers a new deployment automatically.

entities.name: Fluid compute

entities.name: Vercel CDN

sources.quote: Vercel's CDN is a globally distributed network that caches content near your visitors, routes requests, and runs compute close to your data.

questions.question: What is Vercel?
questions.answer: Vercel is the AI Cloud, a unified platform for building, deploying, and scaling AI-powered applications, providing developer tools and cloud infrastructure for a faster web.

sources.quote: Vercel is the AI Cloud, a unified platform for building, deploying, and scaling AI-powered applications.

questions.question: How do deployments work on Vercel?
questions.answer: You connect a Git repository (GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket) and every push to your connected branch triggers a new deployment automatically.

sources.quote: Every push to your connected branch triggers a new deployment automatically.

questions.question: What are Vercel Functions?
questions.answer: Vercel Functions let you run server-side code without managing servers, scaling automatically and using fluid compute for efficient concurrency.

sources.quote: Vercel Functions let you run server-side code without managing servers.

questions.question: What does the Vercel Marketplace integrate with?
questions.answer: The Vercel Marketplace provides integrations for AI providers, databases, CMSs, analytics, and storage.

sources.quote: provides integrations for AI providers, databases, CMSs, analytics, and storage.

questions.question: How is Vercel's CDN different from a traditional CDN?
questions.answer: Unlike traditional CDNs that only cache static assets, Vercel's CDN is framework-aware, reading your routing, caching, and rendering configuration at build time.

sources.quote: Unlike traditional CDNs that only cache static assets, Vercel's CDN is framework-aware.

questions.question: How does Vercel relate to Next.js?
questions.answer: Vercel is the native Next.js platform; Next.js is a fullstack React framework maintained by Vercel, and deploying it to Vercel is zero-configuration.

sources.quote: Vercel is the native Next.js platform, designed to enhance the Next.js experience.

questions.question: How do I get started with Vercel?
questions.answer: Deploy your app on Vercel in three steps: install the CLI, add agent support if you use an AI coding agent, and deploy.

sources.quote: Deploy your app on Vercel in three steps: install the CLI, add agent support if you use an AI coding agent, and deploy.

questions.question: What security does Vercel provide by default?
questions.answer: Every deployment uses HTTPS with automatically provisioned SSL certificates and TLS 1.2/1.3 support, plus always-on DDoS mitigation and an optional configurable WAF.

sources.quote: Every deployment uses HTTPS with automatically provisioned SSL certificates and TLS 1.2/1.3 support.

questions.question: How is Vercel Functions pricing calculated?
questions.answer: Vercel Functions are priced based on active CPU, provisioned memory, and invocations.

sources.quote: Vercel Functions are priced based on active CPU, provisioned memory, and invocations.
